included: Managed transportation and transportation management systems
Third-party logistics and contract logistics providers
Freight forwarding, road logistics, and multimodal capacity providers
Regional and mode-specific freight networks that materially shape shipper and carrier choices
scope.inclusion_logic: Included companies that materially compete for shipper transportation spend, freight brokerage margin, managed transportation workflows, contract logistics, carrier network access, freight execution visibility, or multimodal orchestration. The list emphasizes official company pages, investor relations releases, annual reports, and official newsroom pages.
caveats: Reported metrics are not directly comparable. Some companies report segment revenue, some report company-wide revenue, some report gross bookings, and private companies disclose fewer operating metrics.
Where a metric is platform-wide or segment-wide rather than domain-pure, the metric scope is preserved and labeled exactly rather than narrowed.
Financial metrics use the reporting currency and period from the cited source.
scope.selected_domain_rationale: Uber Freight, C.H. Robinson, and Ryder Logistics all operate in freight logistics orchestration: they connect shipper demand with capacity, technology, managed transportation, execution visibility, settlement, and logistics operations. The domain captures the shared operating model better than a narrower freight brokerage or contract logistics label.
